behavior_summary: U8P_BIGWEBEW::gotHit is an on-hit handler that marks the targeted object with a specific info/state identifier (immediate 0x20C) and applies a process-exclude, then immediately destroys the Item instance passed to the routine.
The routine receives a damage parameter which is present but not referenced by the implementation; the actual cleanup (flag + destroy) is the only observable effect.
The mark (set-info) and process-exclude appear to disable or flag the object to prevent further processing before removal, ensuring a clean deletion.
notable_control_flow: Deterministic, linear sequence: set-info(0x20C, target) -> process-exclude(target) -> Item::destroy(target).
No branching or loops observed; the handler performs a single cleanup path when invoked.
The damage parameter is ignored by the script, so there is no conditional logic based on damage in this function.
open_questions: What is the precise semantic meaning of info id 0x20C (what game-visible state or flag does it represent)?

Does set-info(0x20C) target the same object instance referenced by the Item::destroy call in all cases, or could it refer to a related object derived from the item? (disassembly suggests same pointer but exact engine semantics are inferred)
Are there any engine-level side effects of process-exclude beyond preventing further processing (e.g., visual changes, AI stop) that are not visible in the script?
Is the unused 'damage' parameter intended for future use or consumed by higher-level engine bookkeeping elsewhere?

main_function.name: U8P_BIGWEBEW::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Respond to an on-hit event by flagging/disabling the targeted object (via a set-info with id 0x20C and a process-exclude) and then destroying the item instance passed in.
entry_conditions: Event 6 (U8P_BIGWEBEW::gotHit) — invoked when this object/item receives a hit in the game (on-hit trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_BIGWEBEW::gotHit
function_map.role: on-hit event handler that flags an object and destroys the associated Item instance

external_calls.purpose: Remove/destroy the item/actor instance referenced by the routine's item pointer (passed as 'this') so it is removed from the world.
Engine operations that set a state/info identifier (0x20C) on the object and exclude it from normal processing prior to destruction; used to mark/disable the instance before cleanup.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BIGWEBEW::gotHit is an on-hit handler that marks the targeted object with a specific info/state identifier (immediate 0x20C) and applies a process-exclude, then immediately destroys the Item instance passed to the routine.
